key_insight: The paper reformulates hyperelastic tissue mechanics as an edge-strain interaction potential, enabling fast simulations that retain continuum energetic coupling and support active contraction.
engineering_relevance: This offers a computationally efficient alternative to global nonlinear finite element solves for simulating active soft tissues such as the myocardium.
method: Map hyperelastic constitutive laws to tetrahedral edge strains.
Use an edge-based formulation that preserves coupling between neighboring edges, unlike independent spring models.
Introduce active strain via a time-dependent activated reference configuration and enforce incompressibility without volumetric locking.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests across multiple hyperelastic laws, checks energy balance preservation, and validates the approach on a realistic ventricle with literature-consistent shortening and wall thickening.
watch_item: As with any reduced interaction-potential discretization, accuracy and stability may depend on how well the tetrahedral edge representation captures complex geometry, near-incompressibility, and active loading details.
paper_title: An interaction potential method for passive and active dynamics of hyperelastic materials
